highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.139: Agent View & /goal Command (Research Preview)
highlights.summary: Claude Code now has an agent view — a unified list of all your sessions whether running, blocked, or complete — accessible via `claude agents`. A new `/goal` command lets you set a completion condition so Claude keeps working autonomously until it's met, making long-running tasks much easier to manage.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.142: New Agent Flags & Opus 4.7 Default for Fast Mode
highlights.summary: The `claude agents` command now supports powerful new flags like `--model`, `--effort`, `--permission-mode`, and `--mcp-config` to precisely configure how background sessions behave. Fast mode has also been upgraded to use Opus 4.7 by default, giving you better results without changing your workflow.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest and most capable model in the Opus line, which is now the default for Fast mode in Claude Code v2.1.142. If you're using Claude Code, you're already benefiting from this upgrade in fast mode without any configuration changes needed.

highlights.title: How Claude Code Works in Large Codebases
highlights.summary: This Anthropic blog post covers best practices for using Claude Code effectively on large, complex codebases, including where to start and how to orient Claude to your project structure. It's essential reading if you're moving beyond toy projects and want Claude Code to stay useful as your codebase grows.

highlights.title: Show HN: adamsreview — Multi-Agent PR Reviews for Claude Code
highlights.summary: adamsreview is a Claude Code plugin that runs deeper pull request reviews using parallel sub-agents, validation passes, and persistent JSON state — catching significantly more real bugs than Claude's built-in review. It optionally integrates with Codex CLI and can post PR bot comments, making it a powerful drop-in upgrade for code review workflows.

highlights.title: Learn 80% of Claude Code in 10 Minutes (2026 Tutorial)
highlights.summary: This concise YouTube tutorial promises to teach the most important 80% of Claude Code features in just 10 minutes, making it ideal for developers who want to get productive fast without sitting through a lengthy course. It's a great starting point if you're new to Claude Code and want a quick, high-value overview.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial walks you through Claude Code from the very basics all the way to advanced usage in just 20 minutes, making it one of the most time-efficient resources for getting up to speed. Beginners and intermediate users will both find value in seeing the full range of features demonstrated in a single sitting.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.141: Terminal Sequence Hooks & HTTPS Plugin Cloning
highlights.summary: Hooks can now emit desktop notifications, window title updates, and terminal bells via a new `terminalSequence` field in their JSON output — great for getting notified when long-running Claude tasks complete. A new `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_PREFER_HTTPS` environment variable lets you clone plugins over HTTPS instead of SSH, which is handy in corporate or restricted environments.

tips_of_the_day: Use the new `/goal` command to describe your desired end-state before starting a long task — Claude Code will keep iterating autonomously until the goal is met, so you can focus on other work instead of babysitting each step.

If you work in a restricted corporate network and can't clone Claude Code plugins via SSH, set the environment variable `CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_PREFER_HTTPS=true` in your shell profile to switch all plugin installs to HTTPS automatically.

Before asking Claude Code to help with a large codebase, point it to a high-level architecture document or README first — giving it a map of the project structure upfront dramatically reduces context confusion and keeps suggestions accurate across many files.
